name: GKE Cluster Provisioning and Management
description: Create a Google Kubernetes Engine cluster, connect kubectl, deploy workloads, and configure autoscaling.

steps.title: Enable the GKE API
steps.description: Activates the Kubernetes Engine API for the project.

steps.title: Create a GKE Autopilot cluster
steps.command: gcloud container clusters create-auto mycluster --region us-central1
steps.description: Autopilot manages nodes automatically. You only define pods and GKE handles the infrastructure.
steps.tip: Use Autopilot for most workloads. Use Standard mode only when you need node-level control.

steps.title: Get cluster credentials
steps.command: gcloud container clusters get-credentials mycluster --region us-central1
steps.description: Configures kubectl to communicate with the new GKE cluster.

steps.title: Deploy a workload
steps.command: kubectl create deployment hello-app --image=us-docker.pkg.dev/google-samples/containers/gke/hello-app:1.0 && kubectl expose deployment hello-app --type=LoadBalancer --port=80 --target-port=8080
steps.description: Deploys a sample app and exposes it through a Google Cloud Load Balancer.

steps.title: Check the external IP
steps.description: Waits for the LoadBalancer to provision an external IP address.

steps.title: Scale the deployment
steps.description: Scales the deployment to three replicas for higher availability.

fullScript:
# GKE cluster provisioning and management
gcloud services enable container.googleapis.com
gcloud container clusters create-auto mycluster --region us-central1
gcloud container clusters get-credentials mycluster --region us-central1
kubectl create deployment hello-app --image=us-docker.pkg.dev/google-samples/containers/gke/hello-app:1.0
kubectl expose deployment hello-app --type=LoadBalancer --port=80 --target-port=8080
kubectl get service hello-app
kubectl scale deployment hello-app --replicas=3

faq.question: What is the difference between GKE Autopilot and Standard?
faq.answer: Autopilot manages nodes, security, and scaling automatically -- you pay per pod resource request. Standard gives you full control over nodes but requires manual management of node pools and upgrades.

faq.question: How do I delete a GKE cluster?
faq.answer: Run 'gcloud container clusters delete mycluster --region us-central1'. This removes the cluster and all workloads. LoadBalancer IPs and persistent disks may need separate cleanup.
